title: [v4.2] [CI:BUILD] Packit: Re-introduce packit with fix-spec-file action

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *openshift-cherrypick-robot* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign rhatdan for approval by writing `/assign @rhatdan` in a comment. For more information see:[The Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.2/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
hmm, gotta check the permissions issue with the openshift bot.
OK then. LGTM once you get the permissions issue figured out.

@edsantiago @cevich so I got some more knowledge from the packit team.

1. If the PR submitter has write permission on the GitHub repo, the current packit job will automatically trigger.

2. If the PR submitter does not have write permissions, the current packit job can be triggered with `slash-packit production-build`.

This is mainly done because packit wants to restrict `production_build` jobs to `verified` code. However, the `copr_build` jobs can be automatically triggered no matter who submits the PR. Just that the `copr_build` builds on a separate (temporary) copr, but still in mock environments, so we can check for buildability.

So, here's what I'm thinking..

1. Include both `copr_build` and `production_build` tasks in `.packit.yaml`.

2. When people with write access submit PRs, both tasks run.

3. When people without write access submit PRs, only `copr_build` runs, `production_build` remains neutral (EDIT: unless the reviewer / one of us adds relevant github comment)

4. Cherry-picks for release branches via the bot as well as Release PRs can get `production_build` triggered via github comment, unless the bot itself can be given write access. (EDIT: Release PRs will be from someone with write access so both jobs will trigger automatically)

WDYT?  I will submit a new PR on the main branch for copr_build task so we can see how that goes.

I'm not sure I understand the practical difference (for a PR submitter) between the two systems.  Is this trying to do something more than simply verify package build-ability on every PR (and merge)?  In other words, why not   only use the COPR one since that works for everybody?

> I'm not sure I understand the practical difference (for a PR submitter) between the two systems. Is this trying to do something more than simply verify package build-ability on every PR (and merge)? In other words, why not only use the COPR one since that works for everybody?

Yes, copr-build task should stay if we should keep only one and avoid typing `slash-packit foo` often.
Closing this since we're holding off on packit in podman until it's proved itself in other repos.
